target.title: Fix OpenClaw command tool runtime path
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

## User Problem

Nix users declare OpenClaw command-line tools with `programs.openclaw.runtimePackages`, but the old implementation made those tools available through an internal Codex-home profile path:

```text
agents/<id>/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin
```

That was not the current OpenClaw command-tool contract. It also made the package behavior hard to review because the visible Nix option looked like general runtime tools while the implementation depended on a Codex-specific filesystem layout.

## What This Changes

This PR makes `runtimePackages` mean one thing in nix-openclaw:

```text
Nix-managed command tools for OpenClaw instances.
```

It does that by:

- adding runtime package bin paths to the generated gateway wrapper `PATH`;
- rendering the same bin paths into global `tools.exec.pathPrepend`;
- prefixing agent-level `tools.exec.pathPrepend` only for agents that already override it, matching OpenClaw's agent-over-global precedence;
- using `lib.getBin` for package bin directories;
- removing the legacy Codex-home profile linker;
- replacing the custom one-line runtime probe with `pkgs.hello` in the check.

## Non-Goals

This PR deliberately does **not**:

- enable the Codex plugin;
- import the Codex runtime plugin for this check;
- export `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS`;
- export `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`;
- configure Codex app-server launch arguments;
- prove native Codex `command/exec` or turn execution.

The OpenClaw product follow-up is separate: OpenClaw's Codex adapter should consume the existing `tools.exec.pathPrepend` config and project it into Codex `thread/start` / `thread/resume` config. That belongs in `openclaw/openclaw`, not in nix-openclaw and not as a new Nix knob.

## Compatibility

User config stays the same:

```nix
programs.openclaw.runtimePackages = [ pkgs.jq ];
```

The compatibility break is limited to the old internal Codex-home profile path. Pre-1.0 deployments that depended on `agents/<id>/agent/codex-home/home/.nix-profile/bin` should depend on `runtimePackages` / generated `tools.exec.pathPrepend` instead. There is no cleanup activation for that old path in this PR.

## Behavioral Proof

`nix/checks/openclaw-runtime-path.nix` evaluates the generated Home Manager config and runs `nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs`.

The check proves:

- generated `tools.exec.pathPrepend` includes the runtime package bin dir;
- configured user global `tools.exec.pathPrepend` entries are preserved after the Nix runtime tool paths;
- configured agent-level `tools.exec.pathPrepend` entries are preserved after the Nix runtime tool paths;
- runtime packages do not create `plugins.entries.codex`;
- legacy Codex-home profile activation is absent;
- the generated gateway wrapper prepends the same runtime tool path;
- the wrapper does not configure Codex app-server launch env.

The rebuilt focused check printed this proof summary locally:

```text
openclaw-runtime-path proof:
- generated tools.exec.pathPrepend resolves /nix/store/...-hello-2.12.2/bin/hello
- gateway wrapper prepends the same runtime tool path
- runtimePackages do not configure Codex app-server launch argv
```

## CI Wiring Proof

`flake.nix` includes `runtime-path` inside `checks.<system>.runtime-smoke`.

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` builds `runtime-smoke` in both supported-surface jobs:

- `Linux supported surface`: `.#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke`
- `macOS supported surface`: `.#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke`

The same workflow also builds platform activation on Linux and builds/runs the macOS launchd activation smoke. The activation smokes now assert that the wrapper contains the runtime package path and does **not** contain `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_ARGS` or `OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN`.

## Local Proof Run

Run before pushing `3b14915437e6cdc1d8cfffd06ed5c279f82d76366`:

```text
nix fmt --accept-flake-config -- CHANGELOG.md flake.nix nix/checks/openclaw-runtime-path.nix nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/config.nix nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/options.nix nix/modules/home-manager/openclaw/options-instance.nix nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs nix/tests/hm-activation.py scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh
node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-path-smoke.mjs
git diff --check
nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link --print-build-logs .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-smoke .#checks.aarch64-darwin.module-render
nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link --print-build-logs .#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation
nix build --accept-flake-config --out-link result-platform-activation-local .#checks.aarch64-darwin.platform-activation --print-build-logs
OPENCLAW_HM_ACTIVATION_PACKAGE="$PWD/result-platform-activation-local" scripts/hm-activation-macos.sh
nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-path.drvPath
nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.runtime-smoke.drvPath
nix eval --accept-flake-config .#checks.x86_64-linux.platform-activation.drvPath
nix flake show --accept-flake-config --json
nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link --rebuild --print-build-logs .#checks.aarch64-darwin.runtime-path
```

Local ClawSweeper-style review and two independent review agents found no remaining code blocker after the scope was narrowed. The remaining external proof is GitHub CI on the pushed head.
target.author: joshp123
